sourceMovie.title: Stardust
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Stardust works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maleficent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stardust clicked for you, Maleficent (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stardust clicked for you, Aladdin (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Stardust?
faq.answer: Try Maleficent, Aladdin, The Little Mermaid—each shares DNA with Stardust while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Stardust?
faq.answer: Stardust (2007) is best described as Adventure, Fantasy, Romance, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Stardust worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
